mapper.jsonStringBodyContent:
{
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
  "max_tokens": 2000,
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "You are an AI intelligence analyst for a Head of AI at a data center construction company. You have been given a batch of recent articles from industry RSS feeds covering construction and data center technology news.

ARTICLE BATCH:
{{7.text}}

YOUR TASK:
Analyze all articles above and identify the Top 10 AI products, solutions, or services currently being adopted by data center construction companies or real estate construction companies working on data center builds.

SCORING — rank each product on three dimensions, each out of 10:
- ADOPTION (1-10): How many named major contractors or owners are using it?
- ROI (1-10): How documented is the business outcome? Specific numbers score higher.
- RECENCY (1-10): How recent is the deployment or announcement?

For each product return exactly this format:

RANK: [number]
PRODUCT: [product name and company]
CATEGORY: [Schedule & planning | Site intelligence | Safety AI | Document & contract AI | Platform PM | Estimating]
ADOPTION SCORE: [1-10] — [one sentence]
ROI SCORE: [1-10] — [one sentence with specific outcome]
RECENCY SCORE: [1-10] — [one sentence with date]
COMPOSITE: [total /30]
KEY EVIDENCE: [2-3 sentences: what it does, who uses it, what outcome is documented]
NAMED CONTRACTORS: [list named GCs or owners with confirmed deployments]
SOURCE: [publication name, URL, date]
SOURCE CREDIBILITY: [Independently reported | Vendor-published only]

After the Top 10 list, add a section called COMPANY INTELLIGENCE with this format for each company you find evidence of AI deployment:

COMPANY: [name]
PROCESS: [specific workflow being automated]
TOOL: [specific product]
OUTCOME: [measurable result if available]
SOURCE: [publication, URL, date]

RULES:
- Only include products directly used in construction delivery: scheduling, site monitoring, safety, document AI, estimating, BIM, field operations.
- Do not include data center operations tools used post-construction.
- Flag all vendor-published figures clearly.
- Do not fabricate deployments. If an article does not confirm a named deployment, do not include it.
- Plain English throughout. No jargon. No superlatives.
- If fewer than 10 products are found in the article batch, return what you find and note the count."
    }
  ]
}

contentType.label: application/jsonEnter data in the JSON format, as a string or using a data structure.

inputMethod.label: JSON stringEnter the JSON body as a raw text string. If values contain JSON reserved characters, you must escape them manually.
